sandbox_1775317203861_alutj2dp7.name: fix-security-1775317203860
sandbox_1775317203861_alutj2dp7.description: Automated fix for: Consider adding security scanning to your CI/CD pipeline

features.name: Fix: Consider adding security scanning to your CI/CD pipeline
features.description:
The fix involves integrating security scanning tools directly into the CI/CD pipeline. This means configuring dedicated steps within the pipeline to automatically execute various security analyses against the codebase as part of the continuous integration and continuous delivery process. These analyses typically include: 

1.  **Static Application Security Testing (SAST):** Scans source code, byte code, or binary code to identify security vulnerabilities without executing the code.
2.  **Software Composition Analysis (SCA):** Identifies known vulnerabilities in third-party libraries, open-source components, and dependencies used in the project.
3.  **Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST):** Analyzes a running application for vulnerabilities by simulating attacks from the outside.

By automating these scans, security flaws can be detected early in the development lifecycle, before they are deployed to production.

features.description: Integrate a security scanning step (e.g., Static Application Security Testing - SAST) into the existing CI/CD pipeline. This new step will run security scans on the codebase during the pipeline execution, identifying potential vulnerabilities before deployment.

sandbox_1775364229027_9kzk5bpb0.description: Automated fix for: Consider adding security scanning to your CI/CD pipeline

features.name: Fix: Consider adding security scanning to your CI/CD pipeline
features.description: The fix involves integrating security scanning tools directly into the existing CI/CD pipeline. This typically means adding one or more new stages or jobs dedicated to executing security checks such as Static Application Security Testing (SAST), Software Composition Analysis (SCA) for dependency vulnerabilities, and potentially Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST) or container image scanning, before the deployment phase. This ensures that security vulnerabilities are automatically identified as part of every code change and build.

sandbox_1775375054486_wc8yn3uvk.name: fix-security-1775375054486
sandbox_1775375054486_wc8yn3uvk.description: Automated fix for: Consider adding security scanning to your CI/CD pipeline

features.name: Fix: Consider adding security scanning to your CI/CD pipeline
features.description: The fix involves integrating a Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tool, specifically GitHub's CodeQL, into the CI/CD pipeline. This is achieved by adding a new job, `security-scan`, to an existing GitHub Actions workflow. This job will checkout the repository, initialize CodeQL for specified programming languages, and then perform a security analysis, uploading the results to the GitHub Security tab. This provides automated security vulnerability detection early in the development lifecycle.

features.description: To address the recommendation of adding security scanning to the CI/CD pipeline, a new stage and job will be integrated into the CI/CD configuration. features.description: The fix involves integrating a security scanning step into the Continuous Integration (CI) pipeline. This is typically achieved by adding a new job or step within the CI/CD configuration file (e.g., `.github/workflows/*.yml` for GitHub Actions, `Jenkinsfile` for Jenkins, `.gitlab-ci.yml` for GitLab CI, etc.).
